methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
SpaceForest is a public-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rafał Przybyła that has raised capital via IPO and ESA funding. The valuation landscape for this sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Two primary valuation methods were assessed. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, zero met narrow screening criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier), falling short of the minimum 3-datapoint threshold. Public-comp anchoring was similarly not feasible; the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S stands at 49.8x across the 6 public tickers, but SpaceForest's revenue was not on file, precluding application of this multiple.

Given data constraints, no triangulated valuation range can be supported. The core limitation is absence of SpaceForest's revenue or comparable financial metrics. Establishing a defensible valuation would require: (1) disclosed revenue or revenue guidance, (2) identification of at least three sufficiently similar peers in stage and scale, or (3) access to transaction multiples from comparable IPOs or secondary financings in Space & Aerospace. Without these inputs, quantitative synthesis is not possible.
